properties.remark: ***PRELIMINARY*** STRUCTURAL DAMAGE HAS OCCURRED TO AN OUTHOUSE BUILDING IN CORTLAND JUST EAST OF MOSQUITO LAKE. TREES AND WIRES HAVE BEEN DOWNED IN OTHER LOCATIONS. A SURVEY TEAM WILL BE DISPATCHED THIS AFTERNOON.

AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN STARK COUNTY OHIO THURSDAY EVENING MARCH 14TH AT APPROXIMATELY 8:15PM. THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR NAVARRE AND MOVED IN A NORTHEAST DIRECTION. THE 4 MILE INTERMITTENT PATH ENDED WEST OF CANTON IN EASTERN PERRY TOWNSHIP. THE VAST MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THE STORM INCLUDED UPROOTED TREES AND BROKEN TREE LIMBS. THE SURVEY TEAM DID NOTE SOME MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE INCLUDING SHINGLES AND SIDING TO A FEW HOMES. AT LEAST TWO TREES FELL ON HOMES INCLUDING ONE LARGE HARDWOOD TREE.
LARGE TREE DOWN ON HOUSE ON 14TH STREET.
TREE AND WIRES DOWN.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE THAT INDICATED A EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN STARK COUNTY OHIO THURSDAY EVENING MARCH 14TH AT APPROXIMATELY 8:23 PM. THE TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE TO A SILO AND BARN ROOF ON BENTLER AVE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST WHERE CONSIDERABLE TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG EASTON ST NE AND PILOT KNOB AVE NE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH, SOME FALLING ON POWERLINES. TOWARDS THE END OF THE TRACK, A GAZEBO COLLAPSED, THE FOUNDATION OF A GARAGE WAS CRACKED, AND DOORS WERE DAMAGED OR COLLAPESED ON NORTH AND EAST SIDES OF A BARN AND A GARAGE. A METAL ROOF WAS TORN OFF A PORTION OF A BARN. SEVERAL HOMES HAD MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE INCLUDING MISSING SHINGLES AND SIDING.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ALONG CHAPEL ST.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR PILOT KNOB AVE. AND HAHN ST.

AN EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED IN SPRINGBORO, PENNSYLVANIA ON SUNDAY APRIL 14TH AT APPROXIMATELY 648PM. THE TORNADO WAS ONLY BRIEFLY ON THE GROUND BUT CAUSED DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG BEAVER STREET THEN LIFTED NEAR NORTH MAIN STREET AND ELM. LARGE TREES FELL ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO SIDING ON A COUPLE OF HOMES. TELEPHONE POLES AND STREET SIGNS WERE SNAPPED OR BENT.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED EF-0 TORNADO, MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 84 MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF CHARDON-WINDSOR ROAD AND PRINCETON ROAD IN NORTHERN GEAUGA COUNTY. THE TORNADO SNAPPED TREE LIMBS AND DID DAMAGE TO A HOUSE AND OUTBUILDING AS IT MOVED EAST NORTHEAST. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ON THE GROUND TO THE EAST NORTHEAST FOR JUST LESS THAN ONE HALF MILE BEFORE LIFTING.

LAW ENFORCEMENT AND PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICIALS REPORT MULTIPLE ROADS FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE ACROSS THE MANSFIELD AREA AND RICHLAND COUNTY. THERE ARE MULTIPLE REPORTS OF CARS STRANDED AND PEOPLE BEING RESCUED IN HIGH WATER AROUND MANSFIELD. REPORT OF WATER ENTERING INTO BASEMENTS.

NUMEROUS COUNTY ROADS WERE FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER. COUNTY ROAD 109 HAD WATER RUSHING OVER THE ROAD FROM THE KOKOSING RIVER AND WAS CLOSED. WHETSTONE CREEK NEAR MT. GILEAD QUICKLY WENT INTO FLOOD STAGE.

LAW ENFORCEMENT CLOSED THE ROAD NEAR WALLACE ROAD AND MT. ZION ROAD ALONG THE FORK RIVER DUE TO HIGH WATER. THIS AREA WAS IMPASSABLE WITH SWIFT WATER.

A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN FAR NORTHEAST PORTAGE COUNTY SOUTHWEST OF SOUTHINGTON. THE TORNADO BRIEFLY TRACKED NORTHEAST BEFORE TURNING TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST. THE TORNADO DESTROYED SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND PRODUCED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE, ESPECIALLY ABOUT A MILE SOUTH OF SOUTHINGTON. THE TORNADO HAD AN INTERMITTENT PATH ACROSS NORTHERN WARREN BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE TRUMBULL COUNTRY CLUB.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 3 NNE WINDHAM. NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE INDICATIVE OF AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN FAR NORTHEAST PORTAGE COUNTY SOUTHWEST OF SOUTHINGTON AND BRIEFLY TRACKED NORTHEAST BEFORE TURNING TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST. THE TORNADO DESTROYED SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND PRODUCED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE, ESPECIALLY ABOUT A MILE SOUTH OF SOUTHINGTON. THE TORNADO HAD AN INTERMITTENT PATH ACROSS NORTHERN WARREN BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE TRUMBULL COUNTRY CLUB.

A BRIEF, WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF I-271 AND I-480 NEAR OAKWOOD AT 3:23 PM EDT ON JUNE 16, 2019. THE TORNADO UPROOTED/SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES IN THE AREA. THE TORNADO TRAVELED EAST FOR 2 MILES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR GLENWILLOW AT 3:28 PM EDT.

THE TORNADO TRAVELED EAST FOR 2 MILES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR GLENWILLOW AT 3:28 PM EDT.

AN EF1 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN HURON COUNTY ON TUESDAY EVENING APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF DOWNTOWN NORWALK. THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR US20 APPROXIMATELY 0.5 MILES WEST OF US250. THE STORM MOVED NORTHEAST WITH A TRACK LENGTH OF 1.5 MILES BEFORE ENDING NEAR VETERANS MEMORIAL LAKE PARK. THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO MULTIPLE LARGE TREES.
